title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
seo.metaDescription: Warm up with our authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi). This easy, flavorful recipe brings the taste of Turkey right to your kitchen. Perfect for any meal!
longTailKeywords: how to make Turkish Red Lentil Soup
authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup recipe
best Turkish lentil soup at home
seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?
Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh mint and chili oil.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, comforting flavors of this traditional Turkish Red Lentil Soup, a staple in Turkish cuisine.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a truly heartwarming Turkish red lentil soup, celebrated for its smooth, creamy texture and delightful blend of subtle spices. This wholesome and nourishing dish is a staple in Turkish homes, offering comfort and flavor in every spoonful.

instructions.title: Prepare Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Build the Base
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and flour into the onion and garlic mixture. Cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly, to cook out the raw flour taste. This helps to thicken the soup slightly and add depth of flavor.

instructions.title: Add Lentils and Broth
instructions.text: Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot. Pour in 6 cups of vegetable or chicken broth. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Bring the mixture to a boil.

instructions.title: Simmer and Cook
instructions.text: Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the lentils are very soft and have broken down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. If the soup becomes too thick, add the remaining cup of broth.

instructions.title: Blend the Soup
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, you can transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender, being cautious with hot liquids.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chili Mint Infusion
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the butter over medium heat. Once the butter is melted and slightly foamy, add the dried mint and red pepper flakes. Cook for about 30 seconds, stirring until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the spices.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into individual bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the warm chili mint butter over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, which brightens the flavors beautifully.

tips: Always rinse your red lentils thoroughly before cooking to remove any dust or debris and help achieve a cleaner flavor.
The chili-mint butter, known as 'nane ve pul biberli tereyagi', is crucial for authentic Mercimek Corbasi. Do not skip this step as it adds a vibrant aroma and flavor.
For a richer, more complex flavor, you can roast a small carrot and potato with the onion before adding the lentils and broth.
article:
Dive into the heart of Turkish culinary tradition with Mercimek Corbasi, a beloved red lentil soup that warms the soul and nourishes the body. More than just a simple soup, Mercimek Corbasi is a cornerstone of Turkish cuisine, a comforting staple found on tables from bustling city restaurants to cozy family homes. Its vibrant color, creamy texture, and deeply savory flavor make it an irresistible dish, often enjoyed at any time of day – from a hearty breakfast starter to a soothing dinner. If you’ve ever wondered what truly defines Turkish comfort food, look no further than this humble yet profound bowl of goodness.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)?
Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced mer-jee-MEK chor-ba-SUH, literally translates to "lentil soup." While various lentil soups exist in Turkey, the red lentil version is by far the most iconic and universally adored. Originating from Anatolia, where lentils have been cultivated for millennia, this soup embodies the region's rich agricultural heritage and resourceful cooking. It's renowned for its simplicity, affordability, and incredible nutritional value, packing a punch of protein, fiber, and essential minerals. The soup’s appeal lies in its smooth, velvety consistency and a distinct flavor profile that balances the earthy taste of red lentils with aromatic spices, a hint of tomato, and a bright finish of lemon. It’s a dish that evokes warmth and hospitality, often served as the first course of a traditional Turkish meal, setting the stage for more elaborate dishes to follow. Its comforting nature makes it especially popular during colder months, but its light and refreshing qualities, particularly with a squeeze of fresh lemon, mean it’s enjoyed year-round.

## Ingredients for Turkish Red Lentil Soup
The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its straightforward ingredients, which, when combined thoughtfully, create a symphony of flavors. The stars of the show are, of course, red lentils – specifically, split red lentils, which cook down quickly and provide that signature creamy texture. Beyond the lentils, you’ll typically find:
*   **Onion and Garlic:** The aromatic base that provides depth and savory notes.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** A crucial ingredient that lends a subtle tang, a beautiful reddish hue, and umami richness.
*   **Olive Oil or Butter:** Used for sautéing and adding a silky mouthfeel. Butter is often preferred for a richer flavor.
*   **Spices:** Dried mint (nane) is non-negotiable, offering a unique fresh and earthy aroma. Red pepper flakes (pul biber) or paprika (tatlı toz biber) add color and a mild warmth, with an option for heat if using hot varieties.
*   **Water or Broth:** The liquid base, allowing the lentils to soften and the flavors to meld. Vegetable or chicken broth can deepen the flavor profile.
*   **Salt and Pepper:** Essential seasonings to bring out all the other flavors.
A final touch of fresh lemon juice is always served alongside, vital for brightening the soup and cutting through its richness. These simple components come together to create the quintessential Turkish Red Lentil Soup.

## How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step
Creating a delicious Turkish Red Lentil Soup is a remarkably simple and rewarding process. Here’s a step-by-step guide:
1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Begin by thoroughly rinsing your red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any starch and impurities, ensuring a cleaner flavor and smoother soup.
2.  **Sauté the Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat olive oil or butter over medium heat. Add finely chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
3.  **Build the Flavor Base:** Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, pressing it against the bottom of the pot. This step caramelizes the paste, deepening its flavor. Add the red pepper flakes or paprika and dried mint, stirring for about 30 seconds until aromatic.
4.  **Combine and Simmer:** Add the rinsed lentils to the pot, stirring to coat them with the aromatics and spices. Pour in the water or broth, and season generously with salt and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-30 minutes, or until the lentils are completely soft and have broken down.
5.  **Blend to Perfection:** Once the lentils are cooked, remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it reaches a smooth, creamy consistency. If you don't have an immersion blender, carefully transfer batches of the soup to a regular blender (filling it only halfway and holding the lid down with a towel to prevent spills from steam) and blend until smooth, then return to the pot.
6.  **Adjust Consistency and Seasoning:** If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Taste and adjust salt and pepper as needed.
7.  **Prepare the Topping (Nane Yağı):** In a small pan, melt a knob of butter (or heat a little olive oil). Add a pinch of dried mint and a sprinkle of red pepper flakes (pul biber). Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the mint.
8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the aromatic mint and chili butter (nane yağı) over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing.

## Tips for the Best Turkish Red Lentil Soup
Achieving that perfect, authentic taste of Turkish Red Lentil Soup is easily within reach with a few expert tips:
*   **Rinse Those Lentils:** Don't skip rinsing the lentils! This step is crucial for removing excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy and sometimes impart an undesirable flavor.
*   **Quality Tomato Paste:** Invest in good quality Turkish tomato paste (salça) if possible. It has a deeper, richer flavor than some generic brands and contributes significantly to the soup's authentic taste and color.
*   **Don't Skimp on the Mint Oil Drizzle:** The *nane yağı* (mint oil) topping isn't just for garnish; it's an integral part of the flavor profile. The vibrant aroma of the lightly fried mint and chili flakes adds an incredible layer of complexity and freshness that elevates the entire dish.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** For that characteristic velvety texture, ensure you blend the soup thoroughly. If you prefer a slightly chunkier soup, you can blend about two-thirds of it and leave some lentils whole.
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Lentils absorb a lot of liquid. If your soup seems too thick after blending, gradually add more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. It should be thick enough to coat a spoon but still pourable.
*   **Fresh Lemon is Key:** Always serve Mercimek Corbasi with fresh lemon wedges. A generous squeeze of lemon juice brightens the flavors, adds a crucial tang, and balances the richness of the lentils and butter. It's non-negotiable for the authentic experience.
*   **Let it Rest:** Like many soups, the flavors of Mercimek Corbasi often meld and deepen after resting for a short while, or even better, overnight. Reheat gently, adding a splash of water if needed.

## Serving Suggestions & Pairings
Mercimek Corbasi is incredibly versatile and can be enjoyed in many ways. Traditionally, it's served as a starter, but its hearty nature also makes it a satisfying light meal on its own.
*   **With Lemon:** As mentioned, a generous squeeze of fresh lemon juice is paramount. It cuts through the richness and adds a bright, essential tang.
*   **Crusty Bread:** A must-have for dipping! Freshly baked Turkish pide bread or any crusty artisanal bread is perfect for soaking up every last drop of the delicious soup.
*   **Pickles (Turşu):** The sour, crunchy contrast of Turkish pickles (turşu) or even just a few cornichons or pickled cucumbers, pairs wonderfully with the creamy soup.
*   **Side Salad:** A simple shepherd's salad (çoban salatası) with finely diced tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and fresh herbs dressed with olive oil and lemon juice provides a refreshing counterpoint.
*   **Yogurt (Optional):** While not universally traditional, some people enjoy a dollop of plain yogurt stirred into their soup for an added creamy tang.
*   **As a Main Course:** For a light lunch or dinner, serve a larger bowl of Mercimek Corbasi alongside a simple green salad. It’s nourishing enough to be a meal in itself.

## Variations and Dietary Adaptations
One of the many appealing aspects of Mercimek Corbasi is how easily it can be adapted to suit different tastes and dietary needs, all while maintaining its core essence as a comforting Turkish Red Lentil Soup.
*   **Spicier Kick:** If you enjoy heat, simply increase the amount of red pepper flakes (pul biber) used in the soup itself or in the final mint butter drizzle. You can also add a pinch of cayenne pepper during the cooking process.
*   **Vegetarian and Vegan:** Mercimek Corbasi is naturally vegetarian. To make it completely vegan, simply use olive oil instead of butter for sautéing and for the final drizzle. Ensure your broth is vegetable-based, or just use water.
*   **Gluten-Free:** This soup is inherently gluten-free, making it an excellent choice for those with celiac disease or gluten sensitivities. Just double-check your broth and tomato paste labels to ensure no hidden gluten.
*   **Added Vegetables:** While not strictly traditional, some home cooks like to add finely diced carrots or potatoes along with the lentils. This adds extra nutrients and a slightly different texture. Just ensure they are cooked until very soft before blending.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** For a subtle smoky depth, you can add a pinch of smoked paprika along with the regular paprika or red pepper flakes.
*   **Herb Variations:** While dried mint is classic, you can experiment with other fresh herbs like parsley or dill, either stirred in at the end or used as a garnish for a different aromatic touch.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's a culinary hug, a taste of Turkish hospitality, and a testament to the power of simple ingredients. Whether you’re seeking warmth on a cold day, a nutritious meal, or an authentic taste of Turkey, this delightful red lentil soup is sure to become a beloved addition to your kitchen repertoire. Give it a try, and discover the comforting magic of this timeless Turkish classic.
